methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The broader peer universe comprises 8 private companies, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Dronamics, with no direct public comparables available.

Valuation triangulation relied on a single applicable method due to peer set constraints. Stage-progression analysis, using Rainmaker's $25M total raised as a base proxy, applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to next-stage funding. This yielded an implied valuation of $50M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, filtering for matching stage, proximity, and valuation tier produced fewer than 3 datapoints, making median-based inference unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42.5M to $57.5M, with a median of $50M. This range reflects the step-up methodology's result anchored at the median, with a ±15% bound to account for single-method reliance and lack of peer corroboration. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set comps to validate the stage multiplier's applicability to Rainmaker's specific technology maturity and market traction, and insufficient visibility into the company's revenue, unit economics, or path to profitability relative to sector peers. A fuller peer analysis would require additional same-stage datapoints or revenue-based benchmarking to narrow the range.
